_meta.stability: Frozen for the loom-generation lifetime per ADR-002. Shape additions that preserve wire compatibility (new optional response fields) are allowed; breaking changes require a new generation.
_meta.relation_to_classic: Path parameter renamed dep_id → dep_issue_id (loom vocabulary at the URL level). The removed:bool field is unchanged from classic. Idempotent: removing a non-existent dependency between two valid-prefix ids returns 200 {removed: false}, not 404. That edge-absent idempotency is shared with classic; this handler ADDITIONALLY absorbs the KeyError raised when a referenced issue is wholly missing (classic and the CLI/MCP surfaces convert that to NOT_FOUND) — a deliberate retry-safety tradeoff that can mask a typo'd id. The loom-only issue_found:bool field (added 2026-05-30, wire-compatible per the stability clause) is false exactly in that missing-issue case, so callers can tell a typo from a genuinely-absent edge.

shape_decl.rationale: removed is the classic boolean (an edge was deleted). issue_found is a loom-only optional addition: false exactly when a referenced issue does not exist, distinguishing a typo'd id from an absent edge under the idempotent 200. Path uses dep_issue_id since the path parameter denotes another issue's primary key. Idempotent semantics inherited from db.remove_dependency().

examples.name: success_idempotent_no_dep
examples.note: Removing a non-existent dependency between two valid-prefix ids returns 200 with removed=false. This is the only fixture-replayable case (the populated success path requires seeded issues + a real dependency); see the seeded full-lifecycle test for that. Note these ids are non-existent ISSUES, not merely a missing edge between existing issues: the loom handler absorbs the resulting KeyError to stay idempotent here, whereas classic and the CLI/MCP surfaces convert a missing issue to NOT_FOUND. That divergence is loom-specific and intentional; see the handler docstring.
